issues.description: The diff adds the `isConnected` guard but contains no test that exercises the `isConnected: false` path. All three updated mocks set `dom: { isConnected: true }`, so the existing assertions pass with or without the new early return. The guard is effectively untested within the scope of this diff: a future refactor could silently remove `if (!view.dom.isConnected) return;` and no test in this change would catch the regression. This is exactly the 'untested failure mode' pattern — the entire point of the guard is a specific failure mode (stale async response after view teardown), yet that mode is never exercised.
issues.suggested_fix: Add at least one test with `dom: { isConnected: false }` that asserts `view.dispatch` is NOT called after `flushMacrotasks()`, and a companion test asserting `onNavigate` is NOT called in the cross-file branch. (These tests do exist on disk in separate commits 89449c31 and 28a66b55 — they should be bundled with the fix commit so the change is self-verifying.)

issues.description: The new `if (!view.dom.isConnected) return;` line has no comment explaining why the guard is needed here but not in the sibling LSP extensions. hover.ts uses `hoverTooltip(async ...)` and completions.ts returns an async completion source — both are lifecycle-managed by CodeMirror, so their stale responses are silently discarded. gotoDefinition is the only extension that uses `EditorView.domEventHandlers` with a detached `.then()`, which is precisely why this guard is load-bearing. A future maintainer grepping for `isConnected` across editor/*.ts will see it only here and may 'clean it up' as dead code without understanding the asymmetry.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a one-line comment above the guard, e.g. `// Unlike hoverTooltip/completions (CodeMirror-managed), this .then() is detached — guard against dispatch on a destroyed view.`

issues.description: Three tests construct nearly identical `mockView` objects — now each had to be touched just to add `dom: { isConnected: true }`. The same thing will happen again the next time the mock surface grows (e.g. when the line-bounds guard in the current on-disk version required a `lines: 100` property added to the same three mocks). A small `makeMockView(overrides)` factory would turn N-site updates into one-site updates and prevent future mocks from drifting out of sync with the real EditorView API surface.
issues.suggested_fix: Extract `function makeMockView(overrides = {}) { return { posAtCoords: () => 5, state: { doc: { lines: 100, lineAt: () => ({ number: 1, from: 0, to: 10 }), line: () => ({ from: 0 }) } }, dispatch: vi.fn(), dom: { isConnected: true }, ...overrides }; }` and use it across the suite.

issues.description: The `.then()` chain still has no `.catch()`. With the isConnected guard now in place, the handler is more defensive about view teardown, but exceptions inside the success branch — e.g. `view.state.doc.line(location.range.start.line + 1)` throwing RangeError when the LSP response is stale relative to the current document — will become unhandled promise rejections. This contradicts the defensive intent of the guard: we protect against one failure mode (disconnected view) but leave an adjacent one (stale line number) unprotected. Out-of-scope for the isConnected fix itself, but worth a follow-up task.
issues.suggested_fix: Append `.catch((err) => console.warn('gotoDefinition: failed to apply result', err))` to the promise chain, and/or add a bounds check `if (location.range.start.line + 1 > view.state.doc.lines) return;` before calling `doc.line()`. (Both of these exist on disk in later commits c95075db and 3b6656d0.)

comprehensive_reviewer.summary: The `isConnected` guard is functionally correct and addresses a real lifecycle hazard unique to gotoDefinition — it's the only LSP extension using `EditorView.domEventHandlers` with a detached `.then()`, so its async work escapes CodeMirror's managed lifecycle. Placement is right (after the null-check, before `resolveUri` and any view-touching operations), and it correctly protects both same-file `view.dispatch` and cross-file `onNavigate` branches. No blockers. The main concern is test coverage: the diff updates three existing mocks with `isConnected: true` but includes zero tests that actually exercise `isConnected: false`, so the new early-return code path is untested within the scope of this change and vulnerable to silent regression. Secondary suggestions cover a missing 'why' comment explaining the asymmetry with hover/completions, mock duplication across the three touched tests, and the broader lack of a `.catch()` on the detached promise (out-of-scope but adjacent). All findings are suggestions; the fix itself is sound and should land.
